title: Client Database page with photos, YTD paid totals, and appointment client selection

requirements.summary: Add client photo upload in Client Database form with enforced 35:45 aspect ratio cropping before saving.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend provides a photo uploader in the client form and enforces/crops to a 35:45 aspect ratio before saving.
Backend provides methods to set/replace a client photo and fetch a client photo by client reference for the caller.
A saved photo persists and is displayed when reopening the client record.

file_operations.description: Implement a canvas-based image processing helper to center-crop (and optionally downscale) an uploaded image to a strict 35:45 aspect ratio and return an encoded byte array suitable for backend storage.

Create a reusable client photo field for the client form: file picker, preview, and client-side crop enforcement to 35:45 using the helper in frontend/src/utils/imageCrop.ts. Use shadcn-ui inputs/buttons/dialog primitives already present in the project to keep UI consistent. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add the Photo field to the client create/edit form using the ClientPhotoField component; ensure saved photos are shown when loading an existing client into the form and that replacing a photo updates the preview. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks for client CRUD that support passing photo bytes (Uint8Array|null) when creating/updating clients, and ensure the client list query returns records including any stored photo bytes so the form can rehydrate the preview.

requirements.summary: Implement a navigable “Client Database” page with a full client form (including photo) and a table listing all clients; selecting a row loads the client into the form.
acceptanceCriteria: The app has a navigable “Client Database” page reachable from the main navigation.
The page contains a client form for add/edit with fields: Name, Client Reference, Phone Number, Address, Service, Notes, and Photo.
The page displays a table under the form that lists all saved clients for the signed-in user, with one column per client field plus “Paid this year”.
Selecting a row loads that client into the form for editing.

file_operations.description: Create the Client Database page: top section is the add/edit form (Name, Client Reference, Phone Number, Address, Service, Notes, Photo), below is a table listing all clients. Include row click handling to load the selected client into the form for editing, and include create/update/delete actions consistent with backend capabilities.
Register a new route for the Client Database page (e.g., /client-database) in the TanStack Router route tree so the page is navigable after login.
Add a “Client Database” navigation item to the main navigation (desktop buttons + mobile dropdown), wiring it to the new route. Keep existing authenticated-only navigation behavior.
Add/extend client data hooks (list/get/create/update/delete) used by ClientDatabasePage, with query keys suitable for invalidation after mutations.

requirements.summary: Compute and display “Paid this year” per client for the current calendar year, consistent with Dashboard monthly listing “Revenus (Faits et Payés)” logic and aggregated by client reference.
acceptanceCriteria: For each client in Client Database, “Paid this year” is shown for the current year (based on the user’s local current year).
The aggregation is per client reference (not only per client name).
The total used matches the same underlying logic used by the app for “Revenus (Faits et Payés)” in the monthly listing (i.e., consistent with Dashboard calculations).

file_operations.description: Add a React Query hook to fetch the year-to-date paid value per client reference for the current local calendar year using the backend capability intended to match Dashboard “Revenus (Faits et Payés)”. Ensure relevant appointment-related mutations invalidate/refetch these paid-this-year queries.

In the clients table, add a “Paid this year” column that resolves the current local year and displays the YTD paid total per row, aggregated by client reference (not name). Show a sensible loading/empty state per row while values are being fetched.

requirements.summary: Update the appointment create/edit dialog to allow selecting an existing client (Name + Client Reference) and link the selection to the appointment’s Client Reference while still allowing manual entry.
acceptanceCriteria: In the appointment dialog, the user can pick an existing client (displayed as Name + Client Reference) for the client name input.
When a client is selected, the appointment’s Client Reference is set to that client’s reference (and remains linked for searches/uniqueness).
It remains possible to type a new client manually if no client is selected (unless a valid client selection is required by existing validation).

file_operations.description: Expose a client list hook usable by AppointmentDialog to populate selectable client options (Name + Client Reference).

Augment the “Nom du client” section to support selecting an existing client (display label: Name + Client Reference). When selected, populate both nomClient and referenceClient fields. Preserve the ability to manually type nomClient/referenceClient when no selection is made, and ensure existing submit validation continues to work.
